data.title: BTC's dominance has grown for 3rd year, hitting 59.3% in 2025 per CoinGecko, showcasing its strengthening influence in market changes.
data.description: The dominance of Bitcoin in the market has been on a consistent increase since 2023 and has now reached 59.3% in 2025, as reported by CoinGecko. This uptrend highlights the expanding influence of Bitcoin amidst changing market conditions.

data.title: New Qwen3-Coder by Together AI: Advanced agentic coding model with integrated capabilities for engineering workflows, ready for quick deployment.
data.description: Together AI has launched the Qwen3-Coder, a new agentic coding model boasting advanced features to tackle intricate engineering workflows. The device is now ready for immediate deployment. This latest innovation promises to streamline coding processes and improve efficiency.

data.title: SUI price climbs to $3.62, surging 53% due to SEC's ETF review, approaching $4 with mixed signals.
data.description: The stock of SUI is currently valued at $3.62, experiencing a notable 53% increase subsequent to the Securities and Exchange Commission's examination of Canary Capital's proposed SUI ETF. Technical analyses indicate conflicting signals as the stock approaches significant resistance levels.

data.title: Coinbase's Ethereum Layer-2 solution, Base, stands out with high revenue, driven by priority fees and DEX usage, outperforming other leading rollups.
data.description: Coinbase's Base, a layer-2 solution on Ethereum, stands out for its revenue generation thanks to its distinct fee structure and decentralized exchange activity. It has surpassed competitors in the realm of top rollups. For further details, refer to the full article.

data.title: Arbitrum (ARB) price drops 5% to $0.42, key support at $0.32 in focus, despite bullish trend. RSI neutral.
data.description: After experiencing a 5.15% daily decline, the cryptocurrency Arbitrum (ARB) is currently trading at $0.42. Despite this drop, technical indicators point towards the continuation of a bullish trend, as the Relative Strength Index (RSI) remains in neutral territory.

data.title: Shoreline Secrets Mystery Box by Gala Games released with exclusive NFTs, discounted for a limited time from July 24-31, 2025.
data.description: Gala Games has introduced the Shoreline Secrets Mystery Box, which presents exclusive NFTs and discounted offers for a limited time. This special offer is accessible only between July 24 and July 31, 2025.

data.title: Worldcoin's (WLD) price falls by 6.7%, now at $1.10, testing $1.21 resistance level; 25% weekly increase driven by partnerships.
data.description: The price of WLD has dropped to $1.10 following a 6.7% decrease in a single day, despite experiencing a significant 25% increase over the past week. This surge was primarily attributed to the establishment of major partnerships with Razer and Match Group.

data.title: BitMine targets to amass over $2B worth of ETH in a fortnight, eyeing 5% stake in 6M Ether amid competition.
data.description: The board of directors at BitMine, led by Chairman Tom Lee, has announced plans to obtain and stake a minimum of 5% of the overall Ether supply. This would equate to over 6 million Ether tokens.

data.title: Arizona woman sentenced for aiding $17M North Korean IT worker scheme, laundering money for Pyongyang operatives.
data.description: In Arizona, a woman facilitated North Korean operatives in disguising themselves as American IT professionals, enabling them to transfer millions of dollars to Pyongyang through money laundering schemes.

data.title: Economist criticizes Trump's trade deal with Japan as a tax increase for Americans; tariffs raised from 2% to 15%.
data.description: Economist Justin Wolfers is challenging the positive narrative around President Donald Trump's trade agreement with Japan, stating that it essentially amounts to a tax increase for American consumers. Wolfers clarifies that while it may seem like a win as tariffs on Japan dropped from 25% to 15%, they were originally at just 2%, resulting in higher taxes for American importers. The deal's impact on US automakers is also uncertain. Chrysler's parent company forecasts a $2.7 billion loss due to Trump's tariffs, creating further economic uncertainty. Wolfers' statements can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Global steel overproduction driven by China and US tariffs, threatens industry. Prices drop amid surplus, challenging manufacturing.
data.description: The steel manufacturing industry is facing a serious challenge due to a worldwide drop in prices, primarily driven by a surge in production from China. In addition, the imposition of tariffs by the U.S. has compounded the industry's woes. Steel, traditionally regarded as a symbol of a nation's strength, is now struggling to stay afloat in the face of these pressures. This situation has led to concern among industry analysts as steel companies globally are grappling with the implications.

data.title: Hexaware reports Q2CY25 revenue of $382.1M, up 8.6% YoY, with EBITDA expansion of 156 bps, and EPS at INR 6.25, up 37.7% YoY.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, Hexaware Technologies (NSE: HEXT) reported a year-over-year EBITDA expansion of 156 basis points. Their Q2CY25 EPS stood at INR 6.25, marking a significant increase of 37.7% compared to the same period last year. The company, headquartered in ISELIN, N.J., released these financial results on July 25, 2025. Hexaware Technologies is well-known for offering top-notch IT solutions and services globally.

data.title: Ether may hit $4,000 soon, surpassing Bitcoin, says Michael Novogratz from Galaxy Digital. Institutional interest could be the key factor.
data.description: Michael Novogratz, the CEO of Galaxy Digital, has expressed optimism about Ether surpassing Bitcoin in performance within the next three to six months. He believes that institutional interest could be a key factor in driving this outcome.

data.title: GT1 finds large, high-grade Rubidium resource at Seymour Project, Ontario, enriching lithium-focused strategy.
data.description: GT1 has revealed a significant high-quality Rubidium deposit at the Seymour Project in Ontario. This development enhances the company's focus on lithium by introducing critical mineral elements. The resource potential is notable, with high-grade Rubidium deposits identified, which strengthens the overall mineral value proposition of the Seymour Project. This promising discovery underscores GT1's strategic direction in harnessing the potential of critical minerals alongside its lithium initiatives.

The Thai military has condemned what it described as “barbaric acts” by Cambodian forces, stating that the attacks have senselessly taken lives and injured numerous innocent civilians amid ongoing border hostilities.
